Claim: Fordyce's spots are ectopic sebaceous glands presenting as yellowish-white papules on oral mucosal surfaces like the lips and cheeks.  
Evidence: - They originate from misplaced sebaceous glands lacking hair follicles. - Commonly found on the vermilion border of the lip and buccal mucosa adjacent to the oral commissure.  
Answer: <D>
